name: PENSKE AUTOMOTIVE GROUP, INC.
business: Penske Automotive Group is a diversified international transportation services company and one of the world's premier automotive and commercial truck retailers, operating dealerships in the United States, the United Kingdom, Canada, Germany, Italy, Japan, and Australia. It also distributes commercial vehicles, diesel and gas engines, power systems, and related parts and services principally in Australia and New Zealand, and holds a 28.9% ownership interest in Penske Transportation Solutions.
sellsTo: fleet customers (municipalities, corporate accounts, rental car companies)
mining, power generation, defense, marine, rail, and construction sectors

exposures.sentence: Electric Vehicle ("EV") and Emissions Regulation. The U.K. government requires in 2026 that 33% of new cars sold shall be electric vehicles (with limited allowances) and that manufacturers pay significant penalties if such amount is not achieved and also continues to propose a ban on the sale of internal combustion engines in new cars and new vans beginning in 2030, while allowing hybrid vehicles to be sold until 2035 and limited exceptions to the mandate for certain lower volume manufacturers.

We had significant orders for defense and energy systems products in 2025, and we expect demand for these products to remain strong, in particular in providing standby power energy solutions for our data systems customers.

Additionally, our AI-driven service scheduling and reception system at certain of our dealerships enhances the customer experience by providing instant support, reducing wait times, and offering 24/7 assistance, enabling seamless service even outside regular hours.

In the U.S., we believe new vehicle sales overall were positively impacted by continued strong consumer demand in spite of manufacturer price increases in 2025, while several brands experienced supply chain disruption, including Jaguar Land Rover due to a significant cybersecurity incident and Honda due to its inability to fully source chips due to a disruption in production from a trade dispute between a Dutch supplier and the Chinese government.

catalysts.event: Acquired Penske Motor Group, LLC (PMG), representing two Lexus and one Toyota location in California and one Toyota location in Texas.
catalysts.sentence: on November 19, 2025, we acquired Penske Motor Group, LLC ("PMG"), representing two Lexus brand locations and one Toyota brand location in California and one Toyota brand location in Texas, including Longo Toyota, the largest Toyota brand dealership in the U.S.

catalysts.event: Acquired Lexus of Orlando and Lexus of Winter Park in Florida.
catalysts.sentence: In February 2026, we acquired Lexus of Orlando and Lexus of Winter Park, both located in the Orlando metropolitan area of Central Florida.

tamClaims.claim: U.S. new light vehicle sales were 16.3 million units in 2025.
tamClaims.sentence: In 2025, U.S. industry new light vehicle sales increased 2.2%, to 16.3 million units, including a 4.5% increase in fleet sales and a 2.0% increase in retail sales, as compared to the same period last year.

tamClaims.claim: U.K. new vehicle registrations were 2.0 million in 2025.
tamClaims.sentence: Although U.K. new vehicle registrations increased 3.5% to 2.0 million registrations, including a 4.5% increase in retail sales and a 2.6% increase in fleet sales, as compared to the same period last year, the sale of new premium brand vehicles across the U.K., representing a significant portion of our U.K. portfolio, decreased 6.8% in 2025.

tamClaims.claim: North American Class 6-8 medium- and heavy-duty truck sales were 399,796 units in 2025.
tamClaims.sentence: During 2025, North American sales of Class 6-8 medium- and heavy-duty trucks, the vehicles sold by our PTG business, decreased 15.5% from last year to 399,796 units.

tamClaims.claim: Australian heavy-duty truck market sales were 14,296 units in 2025.
tamClaims.sentence: During 2025, the Australian heavy-duty truck market reported sales of 14,296 units, representing a decrease of 16.7% from last year, while the New Zealand market reported sales of 2,231 units, representing a decrease of 35.1% from last year.

tamClaims.claim: New Zealand heavy-duty truck market sales were 2,231 units in 2025.
tamClaims.sentence: During 2025, the Australian heavy-duty truck market reported sales of 14,296 units, representing a decrease of 16.7% from last year, while the New Zealand market reported sales of 2,231 units, representing a decrease of 35.1% from last year.

tamClaims.claim: Global supply chain is estimated at $11.9 trillion annually.
tamClaims.sentence: PTS participates broadly in the global supply chain, estimated at $11.9 trillion annually, and particularly, in the U.S. supply chain, estimated at $2.4 trillion annually.

tamClaims.claim: U.S. supply chain is estimated at $2.4 trillion annually.
tamClaims.sentence: PTS participates broadly in the global supply chain, estimated at $11.9 trillion annually, and particularly, in the U.S. supply chain, estimated at $2.4 trillion annually.

tamClaims.claim: Approximately 10.2 million commercial trucks operate in the United States and Canada, with up to 5.3 million potential opportunities for full-service leasing and contract maintenance.
tamClaims.sentence: We estimate, based on R. L. Polk registration data, that there are approximately 10.2 million commercial trucks operating in the United States and Canada, of which up to 5.3 million could be potential opportunities for PTS' full-service leasing and contract maintenance offerings.

tamClaims.claim: Approximately 16,900 franchised new-car dealerships in the U.S.
tamClaims.sentence: In 2025, sales of new cars and light trucks were approximately 16.3 million units, an increase of 2.2% from 2024, and were generated at approximately 16,900 franchised new-car dealerships.
